claims.title: Class II HLA loci accumulate non-coding substitutions at 9.8–17.9× the coding rate (DPB1 peak); DRB1 non-coding identity against reference is only 40.6%
Genes with known cis-eQTLs show stronger biophysics–expression coupling than genes without — body deformability partial |r| = 0.262 (eQTL+, n=16,692) vs 0.117 (eQTL−, n=820), Δ|r| = +0.145

Per-allele (coding, non-coding) substitution-count histograms at each Class I locus partition into 6 GMM components — three recurring archetypes (near-reference core, tight multi-serogroup lineages, n
HLA-A CpG density positively correlates with mRNA expression — opposite-signed companion of the dG37 anti-correlation
HLA-A whole-allele dG37 anti-correlates with mRNA expression across protein-distinct alleles
FALSIFIED: HLA-A monocyte methylation does NOT track distance-to-nearest-ERV3-16A3_I-int beyond what a random reference point achieves
Cross-run synthesis: HLA-A expression-biophysics correlations replicate at |rho| ~0.7 across two premise sets (exp24 n=69 and exp26 n=19/HLA-A subset)
Underpowered but direction-matched: HLA-A pos-224 (5'UTR -155 from ATG) CG vs TG partition shows lower TPM at CG (Cohen's d ~ -0.93)
Designed negative control: HLA-B whole-allele dG37 does NOT correlate with mRNA expression (rho ~ +0.017, leave-one-out stable)
Five FDR-significant HLA-B 5'UTR polymorphic CpGs cluster within named CRC modules (4 of 5)
HLA-C miR-148a canonical 8-mer site is absent in 0/3,284 alleles, but the 7-mer seed is present in ~25% — partially dissolved by length audit
HLA-A 5'UTR/promoter is dramatically cell-type-specific — monocyte β=0.083 (open) vs T-naive β=0.593 (closed), a Δβ=0.510 inversion that explains the Phase 4c paradox
Architecture: 85-99.6% of substitutions across HLA loci are non-coding (descriptive K-class claim)
Stimulation amplifies (does NOT collapse) the biophysical-expression signal: 10 of 12 locus-metric pairs increase |rho| under stim
HLA coding Ti/Tv = 0.67–1.54 (vs genome coding baseline ~3.0); HLA non-coding Ti/Tv = 0.51–0.73 (vs neutral baseline ~2.0) — the transversion-enriched signature of diversifying selection
Within-protein (2-field-invisible) non-coding biophysical variance is substantial at Class I HLA loci (29–69% at HLA-A; Z-penalty 68.9%) and corresponds to expression-variant alleles at d up to 2.49
LTR fraction negatively correlates with median GTEx expression after GC residualization (partial rho ~ -0.18) — opposite-signed SINE companion
SINE fraction positively correlates with median GTEx expression after GC residualization (partial rho ~ +0.18)
The Phase 1 cell-deconvolution critique of TE silencing prediction is withdrawn — ChromHMM tissue-specific chromatin states and methylation-array CpG counts are structurally different assays, so cell
CoRSIV variably-methylated regions are enriched 1.676-fold at identity-dependent TEs and the enrichment survives GC- and length-stratified permutation
Mutation null: TE density does not predict somatic mutation rate after GC correction (partial rho ~ 0.000 across SNV / indel / SV)
Methylation cell-type variance at TE boundaries is class-specific — LTR/LINE/DNA elements show ~0.019 cross-cell-type variance, SINE only 0.014, with TE-free regions at 0.018 as baseline
Oncoexapted TEs are enriched in the Active surveillance mode (raw OR=3.88) and the enrichment survives GC-matched stratified permutation
Resolution gain at 147bp: curvature -> H3K9me3 AUROC improves over 1kb with bootstrap CI excluding zero
Retro-Age epigenetic clock CpGs are enriched 3.744-fold at identity-dependent TEs and the enrichment survives GC- and length-stratified permutation
Multi-tissue overall: biophysics adds mean delta AUROC = +0.082 to TE silencing prediction across 5 representative tissues
Designed negative control: biophysics adds essentially nothing to baseline for H3K9me3 prediction (delta AUROC = +0.004 in H1 ESC)
Stacking ΔG₃₇ is the #1 feature for TE silencing prediction across 4 of 5 tissues; evolutionary age ranks 10th–14th
Within age-matched TEs (5-15% divergence), DNA biophysics predicts bivalent chromatin in fetal brain at AUROC 0.986

TE silencing pathway choice partitions into two orthogonal regimes — GC predicts H3K27me3 (Polycomb, AUROC 0.678) while age predicts H3K9me3 (KRAB-ZnF, AUROC 0.633); the dissociation magnitude Δ=0.078
109 biophysics-recombination partial correlations exceed |r| > 0.08 after GC control — the genome-wide strong-signal census
Curvature has opposite signs for crossover vs non-crossover rate after GC correction (CO -0.238, NCO +0.064)
Groove width has opposite signs for crossover vs non-crossover rate after GC correction (CO +0.218, NCO -0.202; Steiger Z significant)
Wrapping energy has opposite signs for crossover vs non-crossover rate after GC correction (CO +0.166, NCO -0.125)
Surprise: OLD crossover hotspots are MORE deformable than YOUNG (Cohen's d = +1.73), overturning the naive gBGC stiffening prediction
Old crossover hotspots are GC-enriched relative to young hotspots (Cohen's d = +1.76); the gBGC scar is visible in the material channel
Cancer translocation breakpoints share the recombination hotspot biophysical signature: Fisher OR=2.34 enrichment, biophysics Cohen's d ~ +0.99
Quantization_map (nucleosome-lattice property) ranks #1 in random-forest permutation importance for crossover prediction; GC ranks 8th
Pre-registration falsified: 1 Mb biophysics-vs-recombination is NOT just an isochore rediscovery — observed signals exceed all pre-registered null thresholds
Amino acid biosynthetic cost anti-correlates with expression in E. coli (Δ=0.60 ATP/residue, 2.5%) and B. subtilis (Δ=0.40, 1.8%) — the cross-species validation of the cost-expression gradient
Mean amino acid biosynthetic cost anti-correlates with human protein abundance after controlling for GC, length, disorder, conservation, secretion, and gene age
Cost-expression partial Spearman survives simultaneous control for GC, length, disorder, conservation, secretion, and gene age (|partial r| > 0.03)
HYPOTHESIZED (post-MOD-1 reframe): symbolic-channel selection on codon identity opposes material-channel perturbation on the same mutations
Human cost-expression effect size is 16% of E. coli's — far larger than the naive Ne≈10^-4 prediction, placing human cost selection in the strong-selection regime
COSMIC v3.4 SBS signatures rank by mean thermodynamic perturbation; clinical signatures (SBS1 aging, SBS2/13 APOBEC, SBS4 smoking, SBS7 UV) span the loudness spectrum
Descriptive: human fixed substitutions show ~2:1 transition/transversion ratio, ~3.4-fold enriched over the unselected germline mutational input
FALSIFIED: ELM reservoir at m=5000 does NOT match elastic-net age MAE on GSE40279 5-fold CV
